jobs.title: Senior Product Manager – HR Tech / Recruiting
jobs.location: 100% remoto (empresa dos EUA) - candidatos baseados no Brasil, Argentina, Paraguai, Colômbia ou México
jobs.jd_full: Profissional com mais de 7 anos de experiência em Product Management, obrigatoriamente com vivência em startups e histórico de entrega de produtos com alto impacto e forte orientação ao usuário. Deve ter capacidade de analisar comportamento, conduzir pesquisas, transformar dados em decisões e priorizar iniciativas em ambientes acelerados. Comunicação clara em inglês é essencial. Experiência prévia no universo de recrutamento é um diferencial significativo. Responsabilidades: Definir e conduzir o roadmap de produtos voltados para job seekers e recruiters, coletar insights por meio de entrevistas, métricas e feedback, transformar fluxos de contratação em requisitos e documentações (PRDs, user stories), colaborar com design e engenharia para garantir entregas no prazo e dentro do escopo, rodar experimentos, analisar resultados, iterar rapidamente, coordenar lançamentos e comunicar prioridades, trade-offs e status para stakeholders e liderança.

jobs.title: Senior Product Manager B2B – HR Tech / Recruiting
jobs.location: 100% remoto (empresa dos EUA) - candidatos baseados no Brasil, Argentina, Paraguai, Colômbia ou México
jobs.jd_full: Profissional com mais de 7 anos de experiência em Product Management, com atuação comprovada em produtos B2B e vivência em startups. Deve ter histórico de entrega de experiências de produto simples, eficientes e de alto impacto, além de forte capacidade analítica para interpretar comportamento de usuários, realizar pesquisas e apoiar decisões baseadas em dados. Inglês avançado é indispensável. Responsabilidades: Definir e conduzir o roadmap de produtos voltados para empregadores, realizar pesquisas com usuários, análise de métricas e coleta de feedback para transformar necessidades reais em requisitos claros, PRDs, user stories e critérios de aceitação, trabalhar junto a design e engenharia para garantir entregas dentro do prazo e do escopo, conduzir experimentos, interpretar resultados, iterar rapidamente, coordenar lançamentos e comunicar trade-offs e status para stakeholders e liderança.

jobs.title: Product Manager AI & Automation
jobs.jd_full: Experiência comprovada como Product Manager ou Product Owner entregando produtos digitais, AI, automação ou produtos orientados por tecnologia. Experiência demonstrada em parceria com fornecedores externos, equipes de desenvolvimento ou parceiros de tecnologia. Background em soluções AI/ML, tecnologias de automação, plataformas de fluxo de trabalho ou tecnologia de RH/trabalho preferido. Forte experiência trabalhando em ambientes de desenvolvimento ágil e iterativo. Experiência em soluções de força de trabalho, MSP/RPO, tecnologia de RH ou indústrias relacionadas é um ativo. Responsabilidades: Liderar o design, entrega e gerenciamento do ciclo de vida de produtos e capacidades focados em AI e automação em Pontoon e seus programas de clientes, colaborar com fornecedores de tecnologia externos, equipes de desenvolvimento e stakeholders internos para construir soluções escaláveis e de alto valor que melhorem a eficiência, aprimorem a experiência do cliente e do talento, e desbloqueiem a excelência operacional, preparar documentação, demonstrações, casos de negócios e avaliações de valor, monitorar tendências de mercado e competitivas para informar a estratégia de produto e decisões de investimento, participar de projetos especiais e realizar outras tarefas conforme designado.

jobs.title: Technical Program Manager
jobs.jd_full: Responsável por projetar e implementar aplicativos móveis inovadores usando Swift IOS Native Android Studio e Kotlin, colaborar com equipes multifuncionais para entregar soluções de alta qualidade que aprimorem a experiência do usuário e impulsionem o crescimento dos negócios, experiência de 6+ anos, salário anual entre $112,000/Year a $132,000/Year, dependendo da experiência e outras qualificações do candidato, elegível para o programa de incentivo anual discricionário da Cognizant e prêmios em ações, com base no desempenho e sujeito aos termos dos planos aplicáveis da Cognizant.

jobs.title: Product Owner
jobs.jd_full:
The Product Owner supports translating business needs into functional software solutions. This individual works closely with Product Management and Engineering to help define and deliver product features that are valuable, usable, and feasible. The Associate Product Owner assists in understanding customer requirements and breaking them into actionable tasks while following a structured process within two-week iterations. This role helps maintain a healthy backlog and ensures the delivery of high-quality features aligned with business priorities.

  • Backlog Management: Own, define, and prioritize the product backlog, ensuring it is transparent, visible, and clearly articulated to the development team.

  • User Story Creation: Write clear, concise, and detailed user stories with acceptance criteria to guide the development process.

  • Sprint Planning and Execution: Actively participate in all Scrum events, including sprint planning, daily stand-ups, sprint reviews, and retrospectives, to ensure the team is focused on delivering the highest value.

  • Stakeholder Communication: Serve as the primary liaison between the development team and internal and external stakeholders, providing updates on progress, managing expectations, and gathering feedback.

  • Requirement Clarification: Be available to the development team to answer questions, clarify requirements, and make quick decisions to remove roadblocks.

  • Acceptance of Work: Review and accept completed work from the development team to ensure it meets the defined user story and acceptance criteria.

  • Feature Prioritization: Constantly evaluate and re-prioritize the product backlog based on business value, technical dependencies, and market feedback.

Qualifications

We’re looking for someone with a strong foundation in product ownership and telephony technology, typically gained through 4–5 years of experience in a B2B SaaS environment. Experience in the automotive space is a plus, but not required. What matters most is your ability to work within Agile (Scrum) frameworks, manage and prioritize product backlogs, and translate business needs into clear, well-written user stories. You bring a thoughtful, analytical approach to problem-solving, with a keen eye for detail and quality. If you’re a proactive, entrepreneurial self-starter who enjoys collaborating across teams and balancing multiple priorities, this is the right fit for you. Familiarity with tools like Jira, Azure DevOps, or Asana is helpful as you partner closely with engineering and business stakeholders.

jobs.title: Design Manager, Data Center
jobs.jd_full:
Company Description

Work with Us. Change the World.

At AECOM, we’re delivering a better world. Whether improving your commute, keeping the lights on, providing access to clean water, or transforming skylines, our work helps people and communities thrive. We are the world’s trusted infrastructure consulting firm, partnering with clients to solve the world’s most complex challenges and build legacies for future generations.

There has never been a better time to be at AECOM. With accelerating infrastructure investment worldwide, our services are in great demand. We invite you to bring your bold ideas and big dreams and become part of a global team of over 50,000 planners, designers, engineers, scientists, digital innovators, program and construction managers and other professionals delivering projects that create a positive and tangible impact around the world.

We’re one global team driven by our common purpose to deliver a better world. Join us.

Job Description

AECOM is seeking a talented and experienced Design Manager to be based out of any AECOM office with some telecommuting and expected travel. The qualified candidate will oversee a wide variety of colocation and hyper-scale data center projects from site due diligence, campus planning, conceptual/schematic/detailed design through construction documents, and construction administration. The ideal candidate has experience managing the design of colocation and hyper-scale data centers, has a background in electrical engineering, or mechanical engineering, and possesses exceptional written and verbal communication skills.

This position offers the flexibility of a remote work schedule and can be based from a variety of locations in the US were AECOM has an office.

AECOM is a leader in the global data center consulting industry with robust teams in the Americas, Europe, Africa, Asia, and Australia. In this role, you will have the opportunity to work with our teams around the world on high-profile, fast-paced data center projects for global clients. This is a hands-on, client-facing role with an opportunity for upward career mobility.

Job Responsibilities

  • Lead data center due diligence and design teams to deliver projects that meet the client’s requirements, on schedule and to a high degree of quality. Design disciplines will include architecture, civil, structural, mechanical, electrical, plumbing, security, fire protection, ICT, and BMS/EPMS.
  • Develop and enforce multi-site project and program management plans.
  • Host and attend meetings with clients and external stakeholders. 
  • Proactively manage and communicate change throughout the project both within AECOM and to our clients.
  • Identify problems early and develop creative solutions.
  • Proactively communicate with all stakeholders to provide project updates and resolve outstanding issues.
  • Develop technical and financial proposals for new and add/move/change data center design projects
  • Develop trusting relationships with client stakeholders/buyers

Qualifications

Minimum Requirements

  • Bachelor’s degree in Architecture, Mechanical Engineering or Electrical Engineering, plus ten (10) years of relevant experience or demonstrated equivalency of experience and/or education

Preferred Qualifications

  • Master’s degree in Architecture, Mechanical Engineering or Electrical Engineering
  • Professional Engineer (P.E.) or Architecture License
  • 12 years of relevant experience in design and/or project management
  • Lean Six Sigma Certification
  • Uptime Institute Accredited Tier Professional
  • Active or recent leadership in data center and mission-critical trade organization
  • 12 years in colocation and/or hyper-scale data center design management & engineering
  • Excellent communication (written/oral)
  • Ability to work in dynamic, time-sensitive situations and with minimal oversight
  • Team leadership skills
  • Strong interpersonal and presentation skills

Additional Information

  • Relocation is not available for this position
  • Sponsorship is available for this position

About AECOM 

AECOM is proud to offer comprehensive benefits to meet the diverse needs of our employees. Depending on your employment status, AECOM benefits may include medical, dental, vision, life, AD&D, disability benefits, paid time off, leaves of absences, voluntary benefits, perks, flexible work options, well-being resources, employee assistance program, business travel insurance, service recognition awards, retirement savings plan, and employee stock purchase plan. 

AECOM is the global infrastructure leader, committed to delivering a better world. As a trusted professional services firm powered by deep technical abilities, we solve our clients’ complex challenges in water, environment, energy, transportation and buildings. Our teams partner with public- and private-sector clients to create innovative, sustainable and resilient solutions throughout the project lifecycle – from advisory, planning, design and engineering to program and construction management. AECOM is a Fortune 500 firm that had revenue of $16.1 billion in fiscal year 2025. Learn more at aecom.com. 

What makes AECOM a great place to work 

You will be part of a global team that champions your growth and career ambitions. Work on groundbreaking projects – both in your local community and on a global scale – that are transforming our industry and shaping the future. With cutting-edge technology and a network of experts, you’ll have the resources to make a real impact. Our award-winning training and development programs are designed to expand your technical expertise and leadership skills, helping you build the career you’ve always envisioned. Here, you’ll find a welcoming workplace built on respect, collaboration and community—where you have the freedom to grow in a world of opportunity. 

As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we believe in your potential and are here to help you achieve it. All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines.

jobs.title: Senior BIM Manager, Data Center
jobs.jd_full:
Company Description

Work with Us. Change the World.

At AECOM, we’re delivering a better world. Whether improving your commute, keeping the lights on, providing access to clean water, or transforming skylines, our work helps people and communities thrive. We are the world’s trusted infrastructure consulting firm, partnering with clients to solve the world’s most complex challenges and build legacies for future generations.

There has never been a better time to be at AECOM. With accelerating infrastructure investment worldwide, our services are in great demand. We invite you to bring your bold ideas and big dreams and become part of a global team of over 50,000 planners, designers, engineers, scientists, digital innovators, program and construction managers and other professionals delivering projects that create a positive and tangible impact around the world.

We’re one global team driven by our common purpose to deliver a better world. Join us.

Job Description

AECOM is seeking an experienced BIM Lead to support hyperscale Data Center projects. The role involves close collaboration with AECOM’s global BIM team, serving as the client point of contact for model coordination, file management, and quality assurance. The ideal candidate excels in dynamic, collaborative environments and is adept at managing evolving project requirements.

This position offers the flexibility of a remote work schedule and can be based from a variety of locations in the US were AECOM has an office.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Responsible for providing leadership and direction to assigned project BIM Managers across U.S. Data Center Projects.
  • Responsible for upholding AECOM and client standards across U.S. Data Center Projects.
  • Oversees the creation and management of BIM models for ACS and MEP systems for data center projects.
  • Maintains BIM data formats, templates, libraries, and best practices to ensure consistency and compliance with project standards.
  • Acts as point of escalation for project BIM issues and ensures client BIM requests are properly evaluated and estimates level of effort.
  • Oversees BIM data integrity, ensure adherence to client requirements, and drive continuous improvement across digital workflows.
  • Serves as Technical Quality Reviewer for BIM deliverables for U.S. Data Center Projects.
  • Performs complex assignments involving the use of multiple application platforms and complex systems including Revit software to achieve set results and deadlines.

Qualifications

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree plus 8 years of relevant experience in CAD/BIM drafting with at least 3 years of experience in BIM management for complex building types or demonstrated equivalency of experience and/or education.
  • Proficiency in Autodesk Construction Cloud/BIM 360, Revit, AutoCAD, and Navisworks
  • Strong leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills
  • Creative problem solver with strong analytical and troubleshooting skills
  • Highly motivated, detail-oriented, and adaptable in fast-paced environments

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Data Center experience preferred
  • Familiarity with data center design criteria and industry standards

Additional Information

  • Sponsorship for US Employment Authorization is not available for this role
  • Relocation assistance is not available for this role

About AECOM 

AECOM is proud to offer comprehensive benefits to meet the diverse needs of our employees. Depending on your employment status, AECOM benefits may include medical, dental, vision, life, AD&D, disability benefits, paid time off, leaves of absences, voluntary benefits, perks, flexible work options, well-being resources, employee assistance program, business travel insurance, service recognition awards, retirement savings plan, and employee stock purchase plan.

Our teams partner with public- and private-sector clients to create innovative, sustainable and resilient solutions throughout the project lifecycle – from advisory, planning, design and engineering to program and construction management. AECOM is a Fortune 500 firm that had revenue of $16.1 billion in fiscal year 2025. Learn more at aecom.com. 

What makes AECOM a great place to work 

You will be part of a global team that champions your growth and career ambitions. Work on groundbreaking projects – both in your local community and on a global scale – that are transforming our industry and shaping the future. With cutting-edge technology and a network of experts, you’ll have the resources to make a real impact. Our award-winning training and development programs are designed to expand your technical expertise and leadership skills, helping you build the career you’ve always envisioned. Here, you’ll find a welcoming workplace built on respect, collaboration and community—where you have the freedom to grow in a world of opportunity. 

As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we believe in your potential and are here to help you achieve it. All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines.

jobs.title: Performance Manager &#8211; Northeast Territory
jobs.jd_full:
We are looking for a Performance Manager who will serve as a strategic partner to automotive clients, driving retention, growth, and measurable business outcomes through AI-powered solutions. In this role, you will build trusted relationships, deliver performance insights, and align customer goals with Impel’s conversational AI and marketing automation technology to maximize impact across the customer lifecycle.

  • Serve as the primary point of contact and trusted advisor for an assigned portfolio of automotive clients
  • Build and maintain strong client relationships focused on retention, growth, and long-term success
  • Analyze performance data and deliver strategic insights that drive measurable business outcomes
  • Identify expansion opportunities and execute account growth strategies
  • Translate complex AI concepts into clear, actionable business recommendations
  • Partner cross-functionally with Sales, Product, Marketing, Finance, and Support to deliver client success
  • Manage multiple accounts and initiatives simultaneously in a fast-paced, dynamic environment
  • Travel as needed to support client engagement and business objectives
Qualifications

We’re looking for someone with 3–5 years of experience in Account Management, ideally within a B2B SaaS environment supporting the automotive industry. A strong understanding of how dealerships operate is important, and hands-on experience working in a dealership is a definite plus. You’ve worked with AI-driven solutions and feel confident discussing how technologies like conversational AI can enhance the customer lifecycle and deliver real, measurable impact.

You’re highly organized, detail-oriented, and naturally driven to perform at a high level. You take ownership of your portfolio, stay ahead of client needs, and thrive in fast-paced environments where priorities can shift quickly. A bachelor’s degree in a related field is preferred. This role also requires the ability and willingness to travel 4–5 days per week to build meaningful, in-person client relationships and support long-term success.

jobs.title: Performance Manager
jobs.jd_full:
As a Performance Manager, you will serve as the primary contact for existing customers, focusing on the application of AI technologies within the automotive industry. Your role will involve managing client relationships, retention strategies, and growth initiatives across your portfolio, while showcasing expertise in conversational AI and AI marketing automation to drive industry-specific efficiency solutions.

You will build strong relationships with accounts by delivering performance metrics and insights, positioning Impelâs AI-powered products as industry leaders. By working closely with customers, youâll gain a deep understanding of their business needs and align them with Impelâs advanced AI solutions, including conversational AI and AI marketing automation. To thrive in this role, you should be highly organized, a strategic thinker, an effective communicator, and possess a strong knowledge of AI applications in the automotive industry.

  • Strong business acumen with a focus on AI applications in the automotive industry
  • Mastery of conversational AI technologies and their implementation in customer lifecycle management
  • Expertise in AI-driven efficiency and effectiveness solutions for automotive dealers, OEMs, and marketplaces
  • A strategic thinker adept at leveraging data and insights to strengthen relationships, identify challenges, and implement AI-powered solutions to drive growth.
  • Ability to explain complex AI concepts and their benefits to clients in clear, accessible language
  • Self-driven and adaptable, comfortable working remotely, on the road, or in the field to meet business needs.
  • Ability to work across departments and partner with individuals of varying expertise, including AI specialists and data scientists, to drive successful outcomes.
  • Excellent organizational, interpersonal, and communication (written and verbal) skills with the ability to explain complex AI concepts to diverse audiences
  • Demonstrated ability to establish clear, achievable goals, with a strong determination and commitment to delivering results while being accountable for outcomes.
  • Exceptional time management skills with the ability to handle multiple AI-driven projects simultaneously
  • Capable of thriving in a fast-paced environment, efficiently solving problems with quick, creative solutions through the use of AI technologies.
  • Proficient in Google Suite, Microsoft Office Suite and familiar with AI and machine learning platforms
  • Comfortable working in a fast-paced and dynamic environment driven by AI innovation
  • Positive, customer-first attitude with a focus on AI-enabled solutions
  • Ability to learn quickly and adapt to rapidly evolving AI technologies

Other

  • Maintains confidentiality of work-related issues, records, and company information.
  • Demonstrates a commitment to Diversity, Equity, and inclusion by treating everyone with respect and dignity, ensuring all voices are heard, and advocating change.
Qualifications

The Performance Manager will have 3-5 years of work experience in Account Management, with a strong focus on AI technologies in the automotive industry. The ideal candidate will possess:

  • Expertise in conversational AI and its applications in customer lifecycle management preferred
  • Proven experience with AI-driven efficiency and effectiveness solutions in the automotive sector
  • Exceptional time management skills with the ability to handle multiple AI-driven projects simultaneously
  • A Bachelor’s degree in a related field (e.g., Computer Science, AI, or Business with a focus on technology) is strongly preferred or 3-5 years of relevant work experience in AI-driven performance manager roles
  • Certifications or advanced training in AI and machine learning technologies are a plus
  • Google Analytics experience preferred
  • Up to 70% travel
  • Bilingual a plus

jobs.title: National Performance Manager
jobs.jd_full:
The National Performance Manager (NPM) is an account management position responsible for providing ongoing support to a defined group of National Major Accounts. Primary responsibilities of the NPM are to ensure customer satisfaction, ensure customer understanding of the Impel value proposition, drive revenue growth within their current book of business, and ensure revenue retention of their national clients is maintained.

  • Responsible for retaining an assigned revenue stream from National Accounts.
  • Manage corporate client communications and training activities that ensure client utilization and satisfaction with Impel products and services.
  • Coordinate with the Regional Performance Management Team to ensure corporate goals and expectations are being met at the store level.
  • Ensure effective client utilization of Impel products, SaaS platform, and services by ensuring client corporate and regional personnel can leverage the platform effectively.
  • Achieve retention and growth of revenue through programmatic actions that support client renewals.
  • Consistently achieve agreed-upon activity levels as defined, activities include phone calls, emails, and any additional agreed-upon MBO’s to ensure 90%+ retention of the client portfolio.
  • Ensure all process management, activity documentation, and revenue tracking are leveraged through the SalesForce CRM platform.
  • Assist the team with the development of customer stories, case studies, and client references to be used for client retention and new client acquisition.
  • Conduct regular account reviews with Regional and Corporate teams to ensure customer satisfaction and address any concerns.
  • Collaborate with internal teams including marketing, product development, and operations to align client needs with business objectives.
  • Continuously advance knowledge of the Impel solutions, learning new features, deepening understanding of current features, and helping to teach the same to both clients and colleagues.
  • Demonstrate the values of Impel in everyday interactions.
  • Perform other duties as required.

Other

  • Maintains confidentiality of work-related issues, records, and company information.
  • Demonstrates a commitment to Diversity, Equity, and inclusion by treating everyone with respect and dignity, ensuring all voices are heard, and advocating change.
Qualifications
  • Bachelor’s degree; and
  • Certification in Google Analytics; or
  • A combination of education and work experience is necessary to perform essential functions.
  • Preferred: Knowledge of retail businesses and/or digital marketing.
  • Technical / Functional Competencies and Traits:
    • Consultative sales skills.
    • A positive attitude and highly motivated with a passion to win as part of a team.
    • Google and SalesForce.
    • Ability to achieve productivity goals, including completion of 40+ activities per day and completion of 2+ meetings online or in-person per day.

jobs.title: Team Manager, DTG Octane
jobs.jd_full:
The Team Manager, DTG Octane plays a critical role in training, maturing, and growing the DTG Octane Team at Impel. This position is accountable for dealer relationship onboarding, training, technical support, retention, and growth. Responsibilities include hiring, management, training, and team performance tracking. 

Responsibilities

  • Consistently lead the team to meet and exceed monthly, quarterly, and annual financial and performance metrics.
  • Hire, onboard, and develop Dealer Success professionals by driving consistent hiring, internal and external training, monitoring, and managing best practices to enhance team performance.
  • Lead team training to develop skill proficiency and effectiveness, resulting in increased upsells, portfolio growth, dealer satisfaction, and greater retention.
  • Serve as a Subject Matter Expert (SME) in dealer meetings, presentations, reviews, and other customer contacts.
  • Identify opportunities across the customer base and leverage current business relationships across the organization and with OEM partners.
  • Refine and enhance a strong SaaS customer success model by developing and maintaining effective processes that drive the highest level of customer retention.
  • Develop, execute, and monitor tools to manage all channels of communication with customers while improving productivity.
  • Work in conjunction with Account Management, Marketing, and Product teams to effectively onboard and train dealers on new products and services.
  • Demonstrate the values of Impel in everyday interactions, both internally and externally.
  • Perform other duties as required.

Other

  • Maintains confidentiality of work-related issues, records, and company information.
  • Demonstrates a commitment to Diversity, Equity, and inclusion by treating everyone with respect and dignity, ensuring all voices are heard, and advocating change.
Qualifications

Education and Experience:

  • Bachelor’s Degree; and
  • 6+ years of experience in setting up and developing customer success teams supporting digital marketing and/or software as a service (SaaS) products. Experience in the automotive industry or software for independent distributors/dealers is preferred.
  • Proven track record in developing and implementing processes that drive individual performance, scaling effectively through rapid growth, and implementing customer success training programs that develop consistent performance of Customer Success Associates.
  • A combination of education and work experience necessary to perform essential functions.
  • Proficiency in MS Word, Excel, and PowerPoint; Salesforce; and Zoom.
  • Travel Requirement: 70%

jobs.jd_full:
Start your next chapter at Revecore!   • Career growth opportunitiesâ¯â¯â¯ 
  • An excellent work/life balanceâ¯ 

Location:â¯Remote – USAâ¯ 

As a Product Manager at Revecore, you will help hospitals recover more revenue through advanced technology. You will partner with our business and engineering teams to deliver leading technology that supports our revenue integrity business unit. Duties include analyzing data to find opportunities, developing a roadmap, delivering outcomes, and presenting to senior leaders. 

Role Overview: 

Revecore is a leader in complex claims and underpayment recovery solutions, helping hospitals and health systems optimize reimbursement and strengthen revenue integrity. As we continue to scale our Complex Claims service line, we are seeking a Senior Product Manager to lead transformation across our core claims workflow and operational platform. 

This role owns and drives strategic initiatives across AI, intelligent automation, and UI/UX modernization within our primary claims processing and operational systems. You will be responsible for identifying workflow inefficiencies, leveraging data and emerging technologies to streamline operations, and delivering scalable product improvements that enhance productivity, accuracy, and financial performance. 

As a senior leader within Product, you will: 

  • Own the roadmap for AI, automation, and user experience initiatives within the Complex Claims platform 
  • Lead end-to-end product discovery, business case development, and execution 
  • Partner closely with Engineering, Operations, Data, and executive stakeholders 
  • Mentor and guide other Product Managers 
  • Collaborate across Revecore service lines to drive shared innovation and platform consistency 

This is a highly visible role requiring both deep RCM expertise and strong technical fluency. 

Key Responsibilities: 

  • Partner with Product Management to analyze and define business problems across Underpayment Workflows 
  • Perform data research and trend analysis to quantify issues, validate root causes, and support prioritization of enhancement backlog items. 
  • Write well-structured user stories and acceptance criteria that guide engineering teams in designing and implementing solutions. 
  • Document and map system logic, data flow, and dependencies within Revecore’s core platforms. 
  • Collaborate with developers to ensure technical feasibility and alignment with business needs. 
  • Support UAT and production validation to ensure new features meet success criteria and deliver measurable improvement. 
  • Maintain a clear view of system performance and recurring issues to inform roadmap decisions. 

What You Bring 

  • 7–10+ years of Product Management experience, including leadership of complex, cross-functional initiatives within enterprise software environments. 
  • Deep Revenue Cycle Management expertise (required), with strong understanding of complex claims, denials, underpayments, payer reimbursement behavior, and operational claim workflows. 
  • Demonstrated ownership of platform-level initiatives involving AI, automation, or workflow transformation, with measurable operational or financial impact. 
  • Advanced SQL proficiency (required) with the ability to independently analyze large relational datasets, define KPIs, validate business problems, and measure product outcomes. 
  • Experience modernizing or optimizing core operational systems and UI/UX within workflow-driven platforms. 
  • Strong technical fluency, including experience with APIs, ETL workflows, financial transaction systems, and claims data formats (e.g., 835/837). 
  • Proven ability to mentor or guide other Product Managers while serving as a strategic individual contributor. 
  • Executive-level communication skills and the ability to influence across Engineering, Operations, Data, and senior leadership. 
  • A strategic mindset paired with hands-on execution — comfortable defining vision and roadmap while also writing clear, testable user stories in Agile environments (Azure DevOps, Jira, etc.). 

Work at Home Requirements:â¯â¯ 

  • A quiet, distraction-free environment to work from in your home.â¯â¯â¯ 
  • A secure home internet connection with speeds >20 Mbps for downloads and >10 Mbps for uploads is required.â¯ 
  • The workspace area accommodates all workstation equipment and related materials and provides adequate surface area to be productive.â¯â¯â¯ 

Employment is contingent upon eligibility to work in the U.S., employment history verification, and a background check.â¯â¯ 
Revecore is an equal opportunity employer that does not discriminate based on race, color, religion, sex or gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, age, disability status, veteran status, genetic information, or any other legally protected status. We believe that a diverse workforce fosters innovation and creativity, enriches our culture, and enables us to better serve the needs of our clients and communities. We welcome and encourage individuals of all backgrounds, perspectives, and abilities to apply.â¯ 
Must reside in the United Statesâ¯within one of the states listed below:â¯â¯ 
Alabama, Arkansas, Florida, Georgia, Indiana, Iowa, Kansas, Kentucky, Louisiana, Maine, Massachusetts Michigan, Minnesota, Mississippi Missouri, Nebraska, New Hampshire North Carolina, North Dakota, Ohio, Oklahoma, Pennsylvania, Rhode Island, South Carolina, South Dakota, Tennessee, Texas, Vermont, Virginia, West Virginia and Wisconsinâ¯â¯â¯

jobs.title: Multi-Site Field Services Manager
jobs.jd_full:
QUALIFICATIONS:

  • Demonstrates excellent verbal and written communication skills.
  • Excellent organization, problem-solving, and customer relationship skills.
  • Build and maintain effective relationships with colleagues, other business unites, and key stakeholders across multiple internal and external departments.
  • Strong organizational skills, with the ability to identify and prioritize multiple tasks.
  • Identifies potential areas of concern, including compliance, quality control, and vulnerabilities and risk.
  • Provides a focus on Service Level Agreement (SLA) definition and customer satisfaction.
  • Identifies and responds to the critical business drivers and those areas of business need which the current or previous operating model does not adequately support.
  • Ability to review, comprehend, analyze, and adhere to Partner SOWs, understanding or project deliverables, financial alignment, and project scoping.
  • Keen understanding of resource capacity planning, forecasting, and scheduling.
  • Ability to negotiate mutually agreeable outcomes in complex services engagements with clients, partners, and WWT stakeholders.
  • Competency in analytical problem solving, customer/partner relationships, project management, and business and sales strategy.
  • Skills in developing new relationships with customers and the ability to understand client business issues and needs.
  • Be an aggressive self-starter with ability to build executive relationships, create opportunities, and close business.

REQUIREMENTS:

  • A minimum of 5 year progressive experience in a related field
  • A Bachelor’s Degree or equivalent experience required

Want to learn more about Infrastructure Services? Check us out on our platform: https://www.wwt.com/infrastructure-services

Certain states and localities require employers to post a reasonable estimate of salary range. A reasonable estimate of the current base pay range for this position is $118,000.00 to $148,000.00 annually. Actual salary will be based on a variety of factors, including shift, location, experience, skill set, performance, licensure and certification, and business needs. The range for this position in other geographic locations may differ. Certain positions may also be eligible for variable incentive compensation, such as bonuses or commissions, that is not included in the base pay.

The well-being of WWT employees is essential. So, when it comes to our benefits package, WWT has one of the best. We offer the following benefits to all full-time employees:

  • Health and Wellbeing: Heath, Dental, and Vision Care, Onsite Health Centers, Employee Assistance Program, Wellness program
  • Financial Benefits: Competitive pay, Profit Sharing, 401k Plan with Company Matching, Life and Disability Insurance, Tuition Reimbursement
  • Paid Time Off: PTO and Sick Leave (starting at 20 days per year) & Holidays (10 per year), Parental Leave, Military Leave, Bereavement
  • Additional Perks: Nursing Mothers Benefits, Voluntary Legal, Pet Insurance, Employee Discount Program

We strive to create an environment where all employees are empowered to succeed based on their skills, performance, and dedication. Our goal is to cultivate a culture of belonging that encourages innovation, collaboration, and respect for all team members, ensuring that WWT remains a great place to work for All!

#LI-EG1

#LI-REMOTE

Qualifications

Field Services Manager, Multi-Site

Why WWT?

Founded in 1990, World Wide Technology (WWT), a global technology solutions provider leading the AI and Digital Revolution, with $20 billion in annual revenue, combines the power of strategy, execution and partnership to accelerate digital transformational outcomes for large public and private organizations around the world. Through its Advanced Technology Center, a collaborative ecosystem of the world’s most advanced hardware and software solutions, WWT helps customers and partners conceptualize, test and validate innovative technology solutions for the best business outcomes and then deploys them at scale through its global warehousing, distribution and integration capabilities.

With over 10,000 employees and more than 55 locations around the world, WWT’s culture, built on a set of core values and established leadership philosophies, has been recognized 13 years in a row by Fortune and Great Place to Work® for its unique blend of determination, innovation and creating a great place to work for all.

Want to work with highly motivated individuals on high-performance teams? Join WWT today!

What will you be doing?

The Multi-Site Field Services Manager executes the vision and strategy of the multi-site Services organization. They are involved from the opportunity qualification process through delivery, ensuring quality execution while achieving cost objectives. The Field Service Manager oversees and directs a team of field technicians, both employees and contractors, responsible for onsite service, installation, maintenance, and support of company products and solutions. This role ensures high levels of customer satisfaction, team productivity, and compliance with operational standards across multiple locations.

RESPONSIBILITIES:

  • Execute our Multi-Site Services Vision and Strategy while driving results, leveraging WWT capabilities, and building / maintaining a strong technician delivery team and developing partner relationships.
  • Lead, schedule, and motivate a multi-site team of field service technicians to achieve performance and quality targets.
  • Build and maintain internal cross-functional relationships with global stakeholders involved in multi-site programs and implementations (Domestic and International).
  • Develop and foster mutually beneficial, trusted relationships with selected business partners to grow and support WWTs Multi-Site line of business.
  • Oversee multiple engagements and/or workstreams of large-scale programs. Establish and enforce safety, quality, and efficiency standards for all field operations. Serve as primary point of contact for escalated customer issues, ensuring timely resolution and effective communication.
  • Participate in Internal and External Customer Kick-Off Calls, and onsite services execution.
  • Manage a team of regional field technicians, including but not limited to resource assignment, performance management, ongoing training, and identification / hiring of additional regional resource needs
  • Familiarity with the concepts of wired & wireless surveys and installations, cabling, power, and other technical components associated with infrastructure.
  • Help to drive 100% compliance with WWT process and policies across our landscape of internal and partner technical resources (on-site and off-site) to also include adherence to the Cut Calendar process.
  • Ensure Delivery Excellence by holding selected Multi-Site Partners accountable to all Contractual T&Cs to ensure all Partner contract deliverables are met within the timelines specified in the Statement of Work.
  • Provide 360º support and contribution throughout the multi-site opportunity life cycle from inception to delivery.
  • Work directly with the Engagement Team on presales opportunities to consult on Multi-Site execution in the development of customer project Statements of Work.
  • Act as a point of escalation to resolve multi-site partner-related issues during the project life cycle.
  • In conjunction with selected Multi-Vendor partners, drive the development of Partner Facing Multi-site Playbooks and Training.
  • Support the PMO responsibilities with Internal and partner project resourcing and scheduling activities, optimization of resources, and overall calendar of activities driven by WWT.
  • Ensure partner technicians cycle through WWTs Partner Vetting process and provide support for the EOMs in facilitating the process.
